originalAlarm.description: `__eckey_814` could be null (null value originating from line 1356) and is dereferenced.

sanitizedDescription: `eckey` could be null (null value originating from line 1356) and is dereferenced.
presenceCondition:
(let ((a!1 (and (not _STDLIB_H)
                (not __STRICT_ANSI__)
                (not __need_malloc_and_calloc)
                (not __USE_EXTERN_INLINES)
                HAVE_ECC)))
  (or (and (and (not _STDLIB_H)
                (not __need_malloc_and_calloc)
                (not __USE_EXTERN_INLINES))
           (and (not _STDLIB_H)
                __STRICT_ANSI__
                (not __need_malloc_and_calloc)
                (not __USE_EXTERN_INLINES)
                HAVE_ECC)
           (and (not _STDLIB_H)
                __STRICT_ANSI__
                (not __need_malloc_and_calloc)
                (not __USE_EXTERN_INLINES)
                HAVE_ECC)
           (not SSL_CTX_set_ecdh_auto))
      (and (and (not _STDLIB_H)
                (not __need_malloc_and_calloc)
                (not __USE_EXTERN_INLINES))
           a!1
           a!1
           (not SSL_CTX_set_ecdh_auto))))
model:
(define-fun __need_malloc_and_calloc () Bool
  false)
(define-fun SSL_CTX_set_ecdh_auto () Bool
  false)
(define-fun __STRICT_ANSI__ () Bool
  true)
(define-fun _STDLIB_H () Bool
  false)
(define-fun __USE_EXTERN_INLINES () Bool
  false)
(define-fun HAVE_ECC () Bool
  true)
reasoning: eckey can be null (from SSL_CTX_get_ex_data returning NULL) and is dereferenced at L17 in the else-else branch (HAVE_ECC=T AND !SSL_CTX_set_ecdh_auto). Genuine null dereference. No // ERROR at L17 (the known bug is the eckey memory leak), so this is a new bug. PC simplifies to !_STDLIB_H AND !__need_malloc_and_calloc AND !__USE_EXTERN_INLINES AND HAVE_ECC AND !SSL_CTX_set_ecdh_auto (STRICT_ANSI cancels out). Restrictive library macro !_STDLIB_H makes PC over-constrained — bug fires in all HAVE_ECC=T AND !SSL_CTX_set_ecdh_auto configs.
